carry_overs_resolved.issue: Sofia role mismatch ('QA Analyst' vs 'QA Engineer')
carry_overs_resolved.note: constants.js line 45 now correctly shows 'QA Engineer'. 🎉

carry_overs_unresolved: overflow elements (coffee corner, speech bubble, window escaping floor bounds) — strike 8

suggestions.title: Floor overflow still not contained — coffee corner, speech bubble, window all escape
suggestions.description: office-floor CSS still has `overflow: visible` (Office.css line 84). Coffee corner's chat-badge label 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' visibly clips/bleeds past the right border in both screenshots. Bloberto's speech bubble floats above the floor top edge. Fix: add `overflow: hidden` to .office-floor and clip any intentional protrusions (speech bubble, solo-badge tooltip) using a containing wrapper positioned outside the floor. The solo-badge already uses `position: absolute; top: -14px` — that's the primary culprit escaping the top edge.

suggestions.title: Three visibilitychange listeners still attached — triple-fire risk
suggestions.description: App.jsx has three separate addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...) calls: line 849 (handleVisibilityResume for polling resume), line 871 (handleVisibilityChange for poll interval adjustment), and line 1072 (handleVisibilityChange for tab title animation). All three fire simultaneously on every tab visibility change. Consolidate into a single listener that delegates to all three concerns, or at minimum reduce to two (resume+poll can share one handler since they both react to !document.hidden).

suggestions.title: Inline toast styles on completionToast AND plantToast — move to CSS classes
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1121–1138 and Office.jsx lines 1233–1250 both contain large multi-property inline style blocks for floating toasts. This makes theming, overriding, and reading the code harder. Extract to .completion-toast and .plant-toast CSS classes in App.css / Office.css. The dynamic border/box-shadow color in completionToast can use a CSS custom property: `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.color || '#6366f1' }}` and reference it in CSS.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking still defined inside useEffect — hoist to module scope
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 983 and 992 define `vibeRanking` and `VIBE_LABELS` as literal objects on every useEffect execution (triggered every time teamVibe.key changes). Both are pure constants. Move them to module scope alongside VIBE_COLORS and VIBE_RANK (which are already correctly hoisted at lines 383–390). Zero runtime cost, instant win.

suggestions.title: aria-live region on conditionally mounted elements — announcements unreliable
suggestions.description: Three places use aria-live on elements that only exist in the DOM when their state is truthy: (1) completionToast at App.jsx line 1120, (2) vibe-caption at Office.jsx line 1422, (3) full-sync-banner and full-idle-banner at Office.jsx lines 1263-1264. Screen readers require aria-live regions to be in the DOM *before* content is injected to announce reliably. Fix: render an always-present sr-only div with aria-live, and update its text content — or use aria-live on a stable wrapper and toggle the inner content. The sr-only span at Office.jsx line 1229 (for ping reactions) demonstrates the correct pattern already.

suggestions.title: Duplicate CSS vibe attribute selectors — single-quote vs double-quote rules
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91–95 define .office-floor[data-vibe='X'] with single quotes for border/box-shadow. Lines 97–115 redefine .office-floor[data-vibe="X"] with double quotes adding --grid-line custom properties. Both are valid CSS and browsers treat them identically, but this means each vibe has two rules fighting for border-color and box-shadow specificity. Consolidate into a single set of double-quote selectors that include both --grid-line and the visual properties. Saves ~10 lines and removes ambiguity.

suggestions.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS defined inside idleWorkers.map render callback
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1494 defines the WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS object inside the .map() callback that runs on every render for every idle worker. This allocates a new object and 5 arrays on each render. Hoist to module scope (alongside WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES and similar constants). Same issue applies to the anonymous tooltip map object at lines 1502–1507.

suggestions.title: blobFace() helper defined as inner function inside CharacterAvatar render — recreates every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 180 defines `const blobFace = () => {...}` inside the CharacterAvatar component body. It's a function that returns JSX based on the `v` prop and is called at line 250. Even though CharacterAvatar is memo-wrapped, blobFace is still recreated on every render pass before memo bails. Extract as a standalone component `BlobFace({ vibeKey })` or at minimum extract the JSX into a lookup map keyed by vibe outside the component. This also improves readability.

suggestions.title: Inline styles on lone-survivor-label and worker-card back elements
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1485 applies a large inline style block to .lone-survivor-label. App.jsx lines 369 and 371 use inline style for spacing/opacity on worker history entries. These are fixed presentational styles — move to CSS classes. The lone-survivor-label in particular has 7 properties inline. Create .lone-survivor-label in Office.css and .worker-card__back-entry-icon / .worker-card__back-time in App.css.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast aria-live region conditionally unmounted — same pattern as completionToast
suggestions.description: ShortcutToast (App.jsx line 639) returns null when phase === 'hidden', which removes the entire aria-live div from the DOM. When phase transitions to 'visible', the newly mounted div with aria-live may not announce in all screen reader / browser combos. Apply the same fix as the completionToast: keep the container div always mounted, hide it visually with opacity/visibility, and toggle content only.

suggestions.title: Mobile header too dense — vibe pill, greeting, sync, and theme toggle all fight for space
suggestions.description: On 375px mobile (screenshot confirms), the header-right area stacks the greeting badge, vibe pill, sync button, and theme toggle in a row that overflows or wraps awkwardly. The greeting text is truncated ('Burning the midnight oil, night owl...') and the tab buttons are partially cut off. Consider collapsing the greeting to just an emoji on mobile, hiding the streak badge below the header on small screens, or adding a `@media (max-width: 480px)` rule that reflows header-right into a two-row layout.

suggestions.title: Office floor elements have no consistent z-index layering strategy
suggestions.description: The office floor uses z-index: 1 and z-index: 2 scattered across coffee-corner, office-window, ambient-particles, and vibe-weather. There's no defined z-index scale, which makes it hard to reason about stacking order and introduces risk of regressions when adding new elements. Define a z-index scale in CSS variables (--z-floor-base, --z-floor-elements, --z-floor-overlay, etc.) and apply consistently.

suggestions.title: Two separate ambient-particle layers rendered — one in App, one in Office
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1095-1098 renders 12 ambient particles at the App level. Office.jsx lines 1575-1584 renders 7 more ambient particles inside the office-floor. On the dashboard view, the office particles are hidden but the App-level ones still animate. Consider whether both layers are intentional or if the Office-level particles are redundant. If intentional, document the layering. If duplicate, remove the Office-level set.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)') called inline during render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1173 calls `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` directly inside JSX render. This is a side-effect in render — safe in practice but not idiomatic React. It also means the result doesn't update if the user switches input devices (e.g., connecting a mouse to a tablet). Wrap in a `useMediaQuery` hook or at minimum memoize with useMemo. Same pattern at Office.jsx line 673 for `(hover: none)` in Character.

suggestions.title: Office window element clips to the very right edge and reads as outside the floor
suggestions.description: The .office-window is positioned at right: 2%, top: 40% in CSS. In the desktop screenshot the window widget appears to sit at or slightly beyond the right border. Even with overflow: visible on the floor, the visual framing implies the window should be an interior element, not spanning the border. Consider adjusting to right: 4-5% to give it visual breathing room from the floor boundary.

suggestions.title: Activity log shows worker emoji but falls back to ROLE_EMOJIS then '🤖' — vacant slot entries look odd
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 96: for entries without a matching worker in roster (e.g., system events), the emoji chain bottoms out at '🤖'. System-type entries with `entry.worker === null` show no emoji at all (correct), but auto-generated entries like vibe transitions lack any brand personality. Consider mapping type: 'system' to ⚙️ explicitly and removing the generic robot fallback for clarity.
